note: Needs the claude-in-chrome extension and a locally runnable app; posting evals need a real PR.

evals.prompt: I'm about to change the <SCREEN> page — capture the before state first
evals.expected_output: Affected screen(s) opened in Chrome BEFORE any code change and screenshotted as before-<screen>.png (issue-numbered if an issue is in context); if the app isn't running and starting it is disruptive, the skip is noted explicitly.

evals.prompt: record a GIF of the changed flow and post everything to PR #<N> (private repo)
evals.expected_output: Flow driven in Chrome and recorded with gif_creator (extra frames around each step, issue-named file); matching after-shots taken; repo detected as private so media is attached via browser file_upload on the PR page with a labeled Before/After/Flow-recording comment. On two upload failures, falls back to a text comment with local file paths.
